hypothesis: Introducing aromatic residues at position 5 and hydrophobic substitutions at position 11 will enhance SSTR2-specific interactions with ECL2 (res 192/193/195/197) and TM5 (205/208/209/212), while avoiding off-target SSTR3/5 contacts. This should increase the selectivity margin by differentiating SSTR2 from other subtypes.

critic_feedback_used.hypothesis: The current hypothesis remains valid — SSTR2 selectivity is achievable with strong ddG and optimized off-target interactions. However, structural quality is a limiting factor, and more precise mutagenesis at non-pharmacophore positions is needed to push Δmargin higher.

proposed_changes.rationale: Many candidates likely failed due to structural clashes or poor geometry. Lowering the clash threshold will enforce better packing and reduce structural failures.

To improve selectivity, we need to disrupt conserved contacts in SSTR3/SSTR5 at non-pharmacophore positions. Focusing design on these positions will allow us to tailor interactions for SSTR2 specificity.
